name: Chicken Sauce Piquant
recipeInstructions:
1. The bacon drippings are used for browning the chicken.
2. Cut 20 lbs of baking hens into 2-inch-long pieces.
3. Brown off the chicken while making the roux.
4. Make a roux using the bacon drippings and flour (see Justin Wilson's recipe).
5. Add onions, bell pepper, green onions, parsley, and garlic to the roux; sauté until onions are tender and clear.
6. Add water to the roux to form a smooth paste.
7. Incorporate tomato sauce, stuffed olives, Louisiana hot sauce, and dried mint into the mixture.
8. Add enough water to cover the ingredients well.
9. Bring the sauce to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for about 4 to 6 hours.
10. Serve over spaghetti. This recipe serves 20 to 40 people and can be frozen if not all is used.
